channel.title: KBBQ on Gourmand Syndrome
channel.description: Recent content in KBBQ on Gourmand Syndrome

item.title: Hee Korean BBQ Grill
item.description: Summary Korean BBQ in NYC is often straight forward. If you want to eat it, you go to Koreatown for a safe bet. If you go away from Koreatown, you risk eating somewhere that may not satisfy that lust. Perhaps the meat would not be as good, or the side dishes would be awful. Fortunately this place in Chelsea was actually pretty decent. I definitely want to preface that the ventilation in this restaurant is not good despite the ducts over the grills, so your clothes will smell.

item.title: The Woo
item.description: Summary This is a surprising decent Korean restaurant in the South Village. Unlike Koreatown BBQ restaurants, this place actually has you cooking the meats just like in Los Angeles. The banchan was on the blander side of things. The daikon was a little watery. The kimchi radish was decent but wasn't as pickled as other Korean places. The broccoli was fine. Lastly, the kimchi was decent but again not as fermented as other places.

item.title: Bap
item.description: Summary The food here was surprisingly decent. I usually don't expect many places in Midtown East to be good, let alone Asian ethnic foods. We ordered a pineapple Mocktail and a glass of Hite, which is nothing to write home about. The banchan here was pretty good, although portion size is on the smaller side. You can ask for additional banchan as needed. Here you got kimchi, mixed salad, jalapenos, pickled cauliflower, pickled daikon cubes, and pickled daikon slices.

item.title: Yoon Haeundae Galbi
item.description: Summary The food was actually surprisingly good. I would come back here again for Korean. The galbi was pretty tasty, and I think the price wasn't too expensive I think. The meat quality is good. I've been back here twice already as I enjoyed this more than the other Ktown BBQ joints. Final Verdict I keep recommending this place over other KBBQ places not only because there is less wait but because the food quality is superb.

item.title: Samwon Garden Bbq
item.description: Pretty decent Korean BBQ. Also a bit expensive, and not as good as Let's Meat. Let's Meat

item.title: Let's Meat
item.description: Pretty decent Korean BBQ. A bit expensive, but everything was good.

item.title: Jongro
item.description: Pretty good. No Korean rice wrap, but meat quality was good.
